sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Default Values in INSERT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When inserting data into a table, some columns may have default values defined in the schema. If you omit these columns in your INSERT statement, the database will automatically use the default value for those columns. For example, in the `inventory_items` table, the `status` column has a default value of `'draft'`, and `is_test` has a default of `0`.

```sql
-- This will use default values for status and is_test
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, last_updated)
VALUES ('New Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.50, '2026-02-10');
```

After this insert, the new row will have `status = 'draft'` and `is_test = 0` unless you specify otherwise.

sketchBlocks.title: NULL vs. Default Values
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
If a column allows NULL and you explicitly insert NULL, the value will be NULL, not the default. But if you omit the column entirely, the default value is used (if defined). For example:

```sql
-- Explicitly inserting NULL into notes
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Sample Magnet', 'Decor', 2.00, NULL, '2026-02-11');
```

Here, `notes` will be NULL, but other columns like `status` and `is_test` will use their defaults.

sketchBlocks.title: How Default Values Are Set in Schema
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Default values are defined in the table schema using the `DEFAULT` keyword. For example, the `inventory_items` table has:

```sql
status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
is_test INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
last_updated TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '2026-02-01'
```

This means if you omit these columns in your INSERT, the database will fill in the default values.

quizDraft.title: Insert with omitted default columns
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new row into `inventory_items` with the following values: name = 'Desk Organizer', category = 'Accessories', price = 8.99, last_updated = '2026-02-12'. Do NOT specify the `status` or `is_test` columns. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Omit columns with defaults; list only the columns you want to set.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, last_updated)
VALUES ('Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 8.99, '2026-02-12');

help.concept: When you omit columns with default values in an INSERT, the database uses the default values defined in the schema.
help.hint_1: List only the columns you want to provide values for. The rest will use their defaults.
help.hint_2: Your INSERT should include name, category, price, and last_updated. Leave out status and is_test.

quizDraft.title: Insert with explicit NULL and defaults
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new row into `inventory_items` with: name = 'Sample Coaster', category = 'Kitchen', price = 5.25, notes = NULL, last_updated = '2026-02-13'. Do NOT specify `status` or `is_test`. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Explicitly set notes to NULL; omit columns with defaults.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Sample Coaster', 'Kitchen', 5.25, NULL, '2026-02-13');

help.concept: If you explicitly set a column to NULL, it will be NULL even if a default exists. Omitted columns use defaults.
help.hint_1: Include notes in your column list and set it to NULL.
help.hint_2: Omit status and is_test; include notes with a NULL value.

quizDraft.title: Insert using only required columns
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new row into `inventory_items` with: name = 'Trial Pen', category = 'Accessories', price = 1.99, last_updated = '2026-02-14'. Use only the required columns and let all columns with defaults fill automatically.
quizDraft.hint: Only include columns that do not have defaults and are NOT NULL.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, last_updated)
VALUES ('Trial Pen', 'Accessories', 1.99, '2026-02-14');

help.concept: Columns with default values and NOT NULL constraints can be omitted from the INSERT if you want their defaults.
help.hint_1: Check which columns are required and which have defaults.
help.hint_2: List only name, category, price, and last_updated in your INSERT.

quizDraft.title: Which columns will use default values?
quizDraft.prompt:
Suppose you run this statement:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, last_updated)
VALUES ('Test Eraser', 'Accessories', 0.99, '2026-02-15');
```

Which columns will use their default values? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Look for columns not listed in the INSERT statement.

help.concept: Columns omitted from the INSERT and with defaults will use their default values. The primary key (id) will auto-increment if not provided.
help.hint_1: Check which columns are omitted and have defaults or auto-increment.
help.hint_2: status and is_test have defaults; id is auto-incremented.

quizDraft.title: Keyword for default value
quizDraft.prompt: What SQL keyword is used in a table schema to specify a default value for a column?
quizDraft.hint: It's a word you see in CREATE TABLE statements before the value.
quizDraft.template: The SQL keyword to set a default value in a column definition is: ____

help.concept: The DEFAULT keyword in SQL is used to assign a default value to a column in the table schema.
help.hint_1: Look for the keyword that comes before the value in a column definition.
help.hint_2: It's the same word used in 'status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft''.
